location.technology_ecosystem: Gwalior has a smaller local technology/startup ecosystem than major metros; the institute has an on-campus incubation centre. This is a calculated/inferred context, not an official rating.
location.cost_of_living_band: Low-to-moderate relative to major Indian metros (inference)
location.data_origin: Official address and JoSAA distances; approximate coordinates/times/hospital distance are map-derived and require manual verification

senior_junior_culture.summary: Official AASF/SAC evidence supports organised peer interaction, while public discussions show active senior-access channels. Evidence is insufficient to generalise about every batch or hierarchy.

academic_culture.summary: The brochure documents a 75% minimum attendance expectation. Public discussion suggests a strong technical peer environment but differing workloads across CSE/IT, MSC, EEE and integrated programmes.
positive_themes: Strong coding/technical peer culture repeatedly mentioned
negative_themes: Branch workload and dual-degree policy require direct verification
EEE outcomes are not yet mature

hostel_reality.summary: Current official brochure reports a compulsory residential system with four boys hostels, one girls hostel and 1,700+ occupancy. Room condition, maintenance speed, Wi-Fi and mess quality vary by block and are not reliably established by current independent evidence.
positive_themes: Internet, water, sports and surveillance officially documented
negative_themes: Compulsory 10 PM attendance and mess rules may feel restrictive

club_and_campus_life.summary: Official 2024-25 evidence confirms active technical, cultural, sports and entrepreneurship events; student photo-based accounts also describe energetic fest periods.
positive_themes: INFOTSAV, URJA, Inter-IIIT, Aurora and incubation events
negative_themes: Event schedules can change by year

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Official placement and event material supports substantial coding, hackathon and software-recruiter activity. Student sources praise CS/IT peers but dispute placement scope and express uncertainty for new EEE cohorts.
negative_themes: On-campus/off-campus and eligibility scope should be checked

locality_and_student_life.summary: The campus is residential and green, around 7 km from Gwalior Junction and 10 km from the airport according to JoSAA. It offers quieter campus life but less immediate metro-scale industry exposure.
positive_themes: Markets within a short drive officially reported
negative_themes: Local internship ecosystem is smaller than major technology metros
Evening transport experience not independently verified

overall_satisfaction.summary: Evidence suggests a strong fit for students prioritising computing, peer learning and residential campus life. Students considering EEE or the integrated IT+MBA route should verify curricula, exit rules and company eligibility directly.
negative_themes: Placement scope and hostel restrictions need personal fit assessment

parent_mode_summary.summary: ABV-IIITM is a centrally funded Institute of National Importance with a verified 360-seat 2026 matrix, official fee circular and strong documented placement medians. Families should verify branch-specific outcomes, scholarship deadlines, hostel rules and five-year programme fit.

student_mode_summary.summary: Strongest documented fit is for students seeking computing, mathematics, coding, residential campus and technical events. EEE and integrated-programme applicants should verify curriculum, software-role eligibility and exit rules before choosing.

decision_risks.basis: Official placement history mainly covers mature computing and integrated cohorts; EEE is newer and branch-specific outcomes are not yet available.
NIRF cohort medians, brochure averages and a 90% eligibility-based claim measure different populations and metrics.
Hostel and mess are described as compulsory, with 10 PM attendance and late-entry recording.
decision_risks.severity: medium for students wanting flexible off-campus life

decision_risks.basis: The 2026 circular is subject to revision; MCM and government schemes are competitive and not guaranteed.
Five-year IMT/IMG programmes have different curricula and duration; exit options and current policy require direct confirmation.
Gwalior provides a lower-cost residential environment but less local industry density than major technology hubs.

data_gaps: Current programme-wise NBA accreditation and validity could not be verified from an authoritative current register.
Current NAAC status/grade and validity could not be verified.

Recruiter-specific roles, CGPA thresholds, packages and branch eligibility are not published in the recruiter list.
Current scholarship deadlines and final scheme-specific documents require verification.
Block-wise hostel room allocation, mess menus, maintenance quality and current curfew exemptions require direct confirmation.

Student-experience evidence remains limited and self-selected.
